notes: eu_hosted = the provider runs inference on EU-located servers (the explicit set the product cares about: the hyperscalers' EU regions + EU-native providers). non_us = the operating company is not headquartered in the United States. country is the company HQ. These flags drive the global 'EU hosted' and 'Non-US provider' filters. Keyed by provider NAME (applies whether reached directly or via OpenRouter). hyperscaler=true for AWS Bedrock / Azure AI Foundry / Google Vertex AI (the big-cloud managed catalogs).

AWS Bedrock.note: EU residency via the eu. cross-region Geo profile; ZDR by default. US parent (CLOUD Act).

Azure AI Foundry.note: EU Data Boundary / EU Data Zone. NOTE: Fireworks-hosted models (Kimi/GLM/MiniMax) are excluded from the EU boundary (US-served).

Google Vertex AI.note: EU residency via europe-west / EU multi-region; never the global endpoint. US parent.

Nebius.note: EU regions (Finland/France); opt-in ZDR + no-training; ISO 27001/27701, SOC 2 II. Yandex heritage (now NL-incorporated).

Scaleway.note: FR-sovereign Generative APIs (FR/NL/PL); ZDR by default. Serves Llama/Qwen/Mistral/gpt-oss (open models only).

IONOS.note: DE-sovereign AI Model Hub, OpenAI-compatible. Serves Llama/Mistral/gpt-oss (open models only).

TensorX.note: EU-sovereign serverless per-token API (Dublin HQ, 3 EU data-centre regions, isolated from US hyperscalers). Zero data retention; GDPR/EU AI Act, ISO 27001-ready. Serves GLM/Kimi/DeepSeek/MiniMax/Qwen/gpt-oss — incl. the SOTA Chinese coding models, in-EU.

TrustedRouter.note: EU-based, security-focused router — still being launched, not production-ready yet.

DeepInfra.note: Single global endpoint, US data centers (per own privacy policy); strong ZDR + SOC2/ISO27001 but no self-serve EU region. (One 3rd-party blog claims EU GPUs — unconfirmed, contradicted by DeepInfra's own docs.)

Together.note: EU footprint (Sweden, since Sep 2025) is GPU clusters / dedicated endpoints only; the per-token serverless API has no EU region selector and runs from US capacity. US HQ (CLOUD Act). Not an EU serverless price source.

Fireworks.note: EU only via dedicated (Frankfurt/Iceland) / BYOC GPU deployments, not serverless.

Parasail.note: US-HQ (San Mateo, CA). Single global auto-routed serverless endpoint, no EU region pin; region handling is a paid Dedicated/sales feature only.

Cloudflare.note: Self-serve Workers AI uses global anycast to nearest GPU, no EU pinning; EU/region-locked inference is Enterprise-only via Data Localization Suite / Custom Regions ("not yet self-serve").

Venice.note: Privacy-first / no-logs but US (per own privacy policy the Service is US-hosted); compute is a global decentralized GPU pool (Akash/Phala/etc.) with no selectable/guaranteed EU geography. No-logs != EU residency.

Novita.note: US-HQ, AWS-backed global per-token API; single endpoint api.novita.ai, no region param. GDPR-compliance claims only, no EU region for self-serve serverless.

AtlasCloud.note: Public per-token API is US/global with no region selection; an eu-west region exists only for deploy-your-own dedicated serverless endpoints (out of scope).

WandB.note: CoreWeave-powered serverless is US-only (multi-tenant on GCP North America, only coreweave-us storage); EU exists solely for sales-gated Dedicated Cloud. CoreWeave (US) owned.

Phala.note: TEE/confidential but US/India nodes, not EU-resident.

Chutes.note: TEE models available; node location not EU-pinned.

DigitalOcean.note: Serverless inference is US-only (single global endpoint inference.do-ai.run, no region param); EU regions run only NA-restricted Dedicated/Agent products. DO states GDPR EU inference regions are roadmap, not shipped (re-check 2026-H2).

Mistral.note: FR-resident first-party API; own (open-weight) models only — Large/Medium/Magistral/Codestral/Devstral.
